No, I’ve never had anything like this happen before. And no one in my family has had fainting spells either.

I was standing when it happened. I had just finished using the bathroom and was about to leave when I felt lightheaded and then blacked out.

No, I don’t have any medical conditions like diabetes or heart problems, and I’m not on any medications. I’m pretty healthy overall.

Can you describe the lesion for me? What does it look like, and when did you first notice it?

I first noticed it about 2 months ago. It’s a small, dark spot on my chest, and I think it’s gotten a bit bigger since then. It doesn’t hurt or itch at all.

Has the color or shape of the lesion changed over the past two months? Have you noticed any new symptoms like bleeding, crusting, or changes in the surrounding skin?

The color hasn't really changed, but it seems a bit more raised now. No bleeding or crusting, and the skin around it looks normal.

Do you have a family history of skin cancer or any other significant medical conditions? Also, how much sun exposure do you typically get?

No family history of skin cancer that I know of. I do spend some time outdoors, mostly gardening, but I try to wear a hat and sunscreen.

I’d like to take a closer look at the lesion. Can you show me where it is on your chest?

Sure, it’s right here on my chest, just below my left collarbone. It’s this small, dark spot that’s a bit raised.

I see the lesion you’re referring to. It’s a small, dark, and slightly raised spot. I’ll need to examine it closely. I’m going to document its size, shape, and color. Could you tell me if you’ve ever had any similar lesions removed or if you’ve noticed any other changes on your skin?

No, I haven’t had any similar lesions removed before. I haven’t noticed any other changes on my skin either. Just this one spot.

I’d like to measure the lesion and note its exact dimensions. Can you tell me if the spot has ever been exposed to any irritants or if you’ve applied any creams or lotions to it?

I haven’t applied any creams or lotions to it, and it hasn’t been exposed to any irritants that I can think of. It’s mostly been covered by my clothes.

I’m going to measure the lesion and document its exact dimensions. Based on what you’ve told me, I’m concerned about the possibility of a skin cancer, such as melanoma. I’d like to perform a biopsy to confirm. REQUEST TEST: Biopsy of the chest lesion.

Not really, but I did try cutting back on fluids and taking some NSAIDs, but that didn’t help much.

I have bipolar disorder, which I manage with lithium. No surgeries or other medical conditions that I think are related. Besides NSAIDs, I haven’t really tried anything else.

I've had the wrist pain for about 3 days now, and it got worse this morning. The pain is pretty bad, especially when I move my wrist or touch it. The skin lesions are these purple, fluid-filled bumps that don't hurt, but they look pretty awful.

THOOTH: The patient has had wrist pain for 3 days, which worsened recently, and describes purple, fluid-filled skin lesions that are non-painful. This could suggest an infectious or inflammatory process. I need more details about the onset and progression of symptoms.
ACTION: Have you noticed any swelling or redness around your wrist? And how long have the skin lesions been present?

Yeah, my wrist is definitely swollen and really red, especially on the left side. The skin lesions showed up around the same time as the pain, maybe a day or two after it started.

Have you had any recent injuries, such as a fall or a cut, around the wrist area? Also, have you been feeling generally unwell, like fatigue or body aches?

No, I haven’t had any injuries to my wrist. I haven’t fallen or anything. I do feel pretty tired and achy, though, like I have the flu.

Have you been in contact with anyone who has been sick recently? Any recent travel or exposure to new environments?

Not really, no one I know has been sick. But I did go on a camping trip to Minnesota last week. Could that have something to do with it?

Did you notice any ticks or insect bites during or after your camping trip? And how has your temperature been? Have you taken your temperature recently?

I didn’t notice any ticks or insect bites while I was camping, but it’s possible I missed something. My temperature has been a bit high, but I haven’t taken it lately. I’ve just been feeling really chilly and not myself.
